issuesFound.explanation: The table header cell "Feature" is part of a header row containing multiple `<th>` elements, creating ambiguity about whether it is a column or row header. Without `scope="col"`, screen readers may not correctly associate it with its column. This violates WCAG 2.2 SC 1.3.1.

issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field has no associated `<label>`, no `aria-label`, and no `aria-labelledby`. Placeholder text is not a sufficient accessible name for screen reader users. This violates WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.2 and SC 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset. Without a visible outline style, keyboard users cannot see when focused elements receive focus, failing WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on features section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, creating readability barriers.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color. Without an outline-style, no focus ring is rendered, making keyboard navigation invisible to users.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible overflow, making content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This fails the intended purpose of the utility and may expose hidden content visually or hide it from assistive technology depending on usage.
issuesFound.fix: Line 498: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) with a transition defined in the base .btn class, creating spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 719: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary custom property is set to #aaaaaa, which against the white background (#ffffff) yields a contrast ratio of 2.3:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1) and is used throughout the interface for paragraphs and descriptions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 102: --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset. Without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border, keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-inset without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only sets outline-offset: -3px while inheriting outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. This leaves no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Screen reader utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible overflow, which makes the content visible on screen and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible. It should use absolute positioning and clipping.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373-382: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion not reduced
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transform transition that is not suppressed under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the sliding animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 423: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. With zero padding, the touch area is insufficient for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-brand without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector only sets outline-color while inheriting outline: none from the global rule. An outline width is required to render a visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset. This removes the focus ring from buttons with no visible alternative, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 419: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Base button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px with vertical padding of approximately 7.5px (space-2-5), resulting in a total height below the 44px recommended target size. This makes the button difficult to activate for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 404: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Small button variant touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of approximately 4.5px (space-1-5), falling below the 44px recommended target size without sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has explicit width, height, and min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1472: width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target at minimum without padding compensation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav link sets min-height: 24px exactly, meeting the absolute minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended size. With no padding compensation, this creates a small touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1522: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Button active state motion not reduced
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies translateY(1px) on :active. This motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 423: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button motion not reduced
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary class includes transform transitions and hover effects that are not covered by the existing reduced-motion media queries.
issuesFound.fix: Add to line 829 media query: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card motion not reduced
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) which is not disabled under prefers-reduced-motion, unlike the base .pricing-card hover effect.
issuesFound.fix: Add to line 1212 media query: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input::placeholder uses --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for text input placeholders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 644: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label uses --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) against --colour-neutral-50 (#f8fafc) background, resulting in insufficient contrast below 4.5:1 for the small uppercase text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 872: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. outline-offset alone is not visible. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not override this rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 338: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. This removes the focus ring for button components when focused via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 436: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size for interactive elements. This makes buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 412: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 498: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 44px minimum recommendation. This creates a small hit area for the search action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 614: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is set to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and significantly below the 44px recommendation. This makes toggling the checkbox difficult for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 902: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility: .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content marked as screen-reader-only will be visible to sighted users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform for its reveal animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 447 is empty and does not disable this motion, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode where box-shadow replacements are suppressed, leaving users with no visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users with no alternative visual indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and no visible alternative (box-shadow or border). Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow is suppressed, making outline the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it entirely leaves high-contrast users without any focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no visible indication of focus. This violates WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a has min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, placing it at the minimum threshold but below the recommended 44px target size. Users may accidentally activate adjacent links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This removes the visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating to the search submit button, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This causes content to remain visible while also being announced, or potentially be removed from layout incorrectly.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target. This may cause activation difficulties for some users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target dimension. This makes the button difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Checkboxes this small are difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative differentiator in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none with no non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) on hover or focus. Colour alone is used to distinguish links, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Since outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline, no visible focus ring renders for keyboard users focusing the scrollable table region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible selectors set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. This completely removes the focus indicator for keyboard users interacting with form fields.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .form-group label uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9) against a blue gradient background of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.7:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) against a background of approximately #5588bb, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px), making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small target.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override. The sliding animation when the link appears may trigger vestibular issues for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition without a reduced-motion override. The transform animation on hover may cause issues for users with motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition that is not disabled in the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 831. The 3D perspective rotation animation persists for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word-spacing can cause words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens character spacing and can reduce readability for users with visual impairments or dyslexia. Tight letter-spacing makes characters harder to distinguish.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none with only outline-offset for :focus-visible, providing no visible focus indicator in high-contrast mode. Box-shadow is suppressed in forced-colors mode, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363-365: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which renders no visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. This removes the default focus indicator for all keyboard-focusable elements throughout the stylesheet, preventing keyboard users from seeing where focus is located.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Inline links in the footer require sufficient vertical space to be easily activatable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-inset without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px without defining an outline width, style, or color. This results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this utility class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 372: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility class and may expose content intended only for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 394-404: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and lacks the minimum 8px padding compensation. This creates a small hit area that is difficult for users with motor impairments to target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 848: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: var(--space-2); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons require sufficient touch area to prevent accidental activations or misses.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-brand without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector inherits outline: none from the global rule and only sets outline-color without specifying outline-width or outline-style. Without a width and style, the outline does not render, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal spacing. This negatively impacts readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making text harder to parse.

issuesFound.title: Search form label hidden from assistive technologies

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-base)), which causes spatial movement when the link appears. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular distress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422: .skip-link { position: fixed; top: var(--space-3); left: var(--space-3); z-index: var(--z-toast); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); border: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); transform: translateY(-200%); transition: transform var(--transition-base); white-space: nowrap; } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on activation (transform: translateY(1px) in :active). The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 461 does not override this transition, potentially causing vestibular issues for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 461-463: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower), causing spatial movement on hover (perspective rotation). The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 993 does not override this transition, which may trigger vestibular distress for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 993-996: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error element to the field's aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as invalid when navigating forms.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` in the markInvalid function after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus management and deactivates it in the close() function, but never creates or activates the focus trap in the open() function. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer into the background content, violating focus containment requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` in the open() function after showing the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput _onApply missing aria-invalid on input
issuesFound.explanation: When discount code validation fails, the error message is displayed and announced, but the input field is not marked with aria-invalid="true" to indicate the error state programmatically.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers with stale state information.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid changes, but there is no announcement to screen readers via a live region to inform users how many results are now showing or that the filter has changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly/annual), the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, so users may not realize the prices have changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown panel, the function removes CSS classes and sets hidden attribute on the panel, but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the content becomes visible but screen reader users are not notified of the state change unless focus is moved. The function should announce the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for the closed state.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel goTo missing focus management on slide change
issuesFound.explanation: When the carousel slide changes, the visual content updates and aria-hidden is toggled, but focus is not moved to the new slide content, making it difficult for keyboard users to perceive the change.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox open function sets internal state to open but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the input element, leaving screen readers unaware that suggestions are available.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox close function clears state but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the input element.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons visually indicate active state via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes programmatically, but the state change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether the menu is now open or closed.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves, but there is no auditory confirmation via a live region that the action succeeded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When initializing the search module, the combobox input gets role, aria-haspopup, and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" for the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _onViewToggle missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) set aria-pressed when clicked, but the initial state is not set when the module initializes, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial pressed state.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter highlightColumn missing aria-live for changes
issuesFound.explanation: When hovering or clicking to highlight a comparison table column, the visual highlight changes but there is no announcement for screen reader users. However, since this is a hover-based interaction and not a persistent state change, this is a best practice gap rather than a WCAG failure.
issuesFound.fix: (No fix required per confidence gate - hover effects are not required to be announced unless they convey essential information)

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render missing live region for filter removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is removed, the filter is updated and the product grid refreshes, but the removal action and the resulting filter change are not announced to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField _addFile missing aria-live for queue updates
issuesFound.explanation: While files added to the queue are announced via liveRegion.announce, the removal of files via _removeEntry also announces, but if multiple files are added rapidly, the announcements may queue up. This is a minor usability issue, not a WCAG failure.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard _goToStep missing aria-live for step content
issuesFound.explanation: While the wizard announces the step change via liveRegion.announce at the end of the function, if the step content is complex, users might benefit from the step panel itself having aria-live. However, the current implementation is acceptable.

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap missing aria-modal on container
issuesFound.explanation: When creating a focus trap for modal dialogs or drawers, the container element should have aria-modal="true" to indicate to screen readers that content outside the modal is inert. The function does not set this attribute.

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap missing aria-modal removal on deactivate
issuesFound.explanation: When the focus trap is deactivated, the aria-modal attribute should be removed from the container.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hide/show missing aria-hidden consideration
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden via CSS classes (scrolled down), if the CSS uses transform rather than display:none, the header remains in the accessibility tree. However, this is typically acceptable for sticky headers that remain accessible via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal missing aria-hidden removal
issuesFound.explanation: Elements with data-reveal may have aria-hidden initially set in HTML. When they are revealed, the code removes the data-reveal attribute and adds the visible class, but does not explicitly remove aria-hidden if it was present. However, if the CSS makes it visible and the element is not actually hidden, this is not a WCAG failure.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _update missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is shown/hidden via the hidden attribute and CSS classes. This is correct behavior and does not require aria-hidden since hidden attribute handles it.

issuesFound.title: productCardRegistry register missing aria-hidden on hidden cards
issuesFound.explanation: When product cards are filtered out, they receive the hidden attribute and aria-hidden="true" in filterAnimation.run. This is correct.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause missing aria-live for state
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause state is communicated via aria-pressed on the button, which is sufficient. No additional live region is needed.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm _onApply missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When email validation fails, inlineValidator.validate is called which sets aria-invalid, but if the honeypot check fails or other errors occur, aria-invalid might not be set consistently. However, the inlineValidator handles the field validation.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers _processNav missing aria-current on last item
issuesFound.explanation: The function processes breadcrumb navigation but the code shown does not set aria-current="page" on the last item's link or span. The comment says "Mark the last item as the current page" but the actual code is cut off or missing.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher set missing aria-pressed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are updated with aria-pressed in the event listener, but if the theme is set programmatically, the aria-pressed state might not be updated on all buttons. The code shows it updates all buttons in the set function.
issuesFound.fix: Already handled in the code.

issuesFound.title: formAnalytics track missing aria-describedby on error observation
issuesFound.explanation:
The mutation observer watches for aria-invalid changes but does not ensure fields have aria-describedby linking to error messages. However, this is handled by the inlineValidator.

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap activate missing aria-modal

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap deactivate missing aria-modal removal
issuesFound.explanation: When the focus trap is deactivated, the aria-modal attribute should be removed from the container to restore normal accessibility tree semantics.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers _processNav missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput _onApply missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` reveals the panel but does not update the trigger's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` hides the panel but does not update the trigger's `aria-expanded` attribute to "false". This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register function does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but fails to set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. The initial state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid helper does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid helper does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` utility function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` configures the search input as a combobox (`role`, `aria-autocomplete`, `aria-controls`) but fails to set `aria-expanded`. The state of the suggestion list is unknown.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce count update

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates prices and savings messages but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual") to an `aria-live` region. Users miss the context of the price update.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hide function does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `hide` function in `stickyHeader` adds a CSS class to visually hide the header but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen readers may still navigate to hidden header links.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent hideBanner does not set aria-hidden during fade
issuesFound.explanation: The `_hideBanner` function fades out the cookie banner (opacity: 0) before removing it from the DOM. During the fade, the element is invisible but still accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing accessible structure

'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an emoji icon (☀️, 🌙, or 💻) without an aria-label. The IconButton interface requires an aria-label prop, but the consuming component does not provide one, leaving screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT
